content:
Hi,

While working on my DRM driver, I’ve often encountered endianness issues, this time around the Register machinery and its register!() macro.
My hardware is mostly big endian (with some weirdness I’ll get to later), with registers being either u16 or u32.
So far I had added a small helper to u32::swap_bytes() right after read and right before write, but now that the register stuff is in I set to replace my helpers with proper register access.
Should I add an Io::write_be_reg() and a Io::read_be() now? In the past, I got recommended to add a wrapper type similar to RelaxedMmio to do big-endian access, is this still the way forward?

As for the weirdness I mentioned above, some registers have two u16 halves which compose a whole u32 address for instance, but are ordered lo first and hi last. Is there a way to represent those as a single u32 register that would split both halves correctly, but expose a single u32 to the user to simplify setting the address?

Thanks a lot for your insight!
